state.title: tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz

input.prompt:
Search the Linux kernel source tree at /tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz for DMA engine drivers that:
1. Read DMA channel count from hardware registers
2. Already have device tree binding support
3. Might need a "dma-channels" property fallback

Specifically:
- Look in drivers/dma/ for relevant DMA controller drivers
- Find files that read a channel count from a register (search for patterns like "dma_channels", "num_channels", "nr_channels", "chancnt", "DMA_CHAN_COUNT", "channels" near register reads)
- Search for any existing use of "dma-channels" device tree property in the DMA subsystem
- Look for drivers that use both device tree and register-based channel detection

Return the following information:
1. The most relevant DMA driver files that use hardware registers to determine channel count
2. Any existing patterns of "dma-channels" property usage in the kernel
3. The specific driver that seems to be the target for this change (most likely a driver that currently reads channel count from hardware registers)
4. Any relevant device tree binding documentation for DMA controllers
5. Show the relevant code snippets (function names and line numbers) where channel count is currently determined

state.output:
<task id="ses_10d7b5d2cffe761u7pBjU3Mout" state="completed">
<task_result>
Now I have a comprehensive view of the entire DMA subsystem. Here is the complete analysis:

---

## 1. Most relevant DMA driver files that use hardware registers to determine channel count

### Primary candidates (read channel count from HW registers, NO `dma-channels` DT property):

| File | Register/Method | Lines |
|------|----------------|-------|
| **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/arm-dma350.c`** | Reads `DMA_BUILDCFG0` / `DMA_CFG_NUM_CHANNELS` (bits 9:4) | **Lines 546-547** |
| **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/pl330.c`** | Reads `CR0` / `CR0_NUM_CHANS_MASK` | **Lines 1835-1838** |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/ti/k3-udma.c` | Reads capability registers at offset 0x28/0x2c/0x30 | **Lines 4519-4541** |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/hsu/hsu.c` | Calculates from IO space length (not a direct register read) | **Lines 440-441** |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/ti/edma.c` | Reads channel count from register value | **Line 2032** |

### Already uses `dma-channels` DT property WITH register fallback:

| File | Mechanism | Lines |
|------|-----------|-------|
| **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/stm32/stm32-dma3.c`** | DT `dma-channels` first, falls back to `STM32_DMA3_HWCFGR1` register | **Lines 1806-1808** |

---

## 2. Existing patterns of `dma-channels` property usage in the kernel

The `dma-channels` property is **defined in the common DMA binding** at:
- **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/Documentation/devicetree/bindings/dma/dma-common.yaml`** (lines 39-42)

It is a `uint32` property described as "Number of DMA channels supported by the controller."

### Drivers currently using `dma-channels` (without hardware register fallback):

These 28+ drivers read channel count only from the DT property, with no fallback to hardware registers:

| Driver File | Line |
|-------------|------|
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/st_fdma.c` | 720 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/pxa_dma.c` | 1365-1367 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/fsl-qdma.c` | 1129 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/stm32/stm32-mdma.c` | 1604 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/mmp_pdma.c` | 1258-1260 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/k3dma.c` | 856 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/qcom/gpi.c` | 2176 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/ti/omap-dma.c` | 1738-1743 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/lgm/lgm-dma.c` | 1443 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/mediatek/mtk-cqdma.c` | 794 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/ti/cppi41.c` | 1100-1105 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/sprd-dma.c` | 1128-1130 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/uniphier-xdmac.c` | 490 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/dw/of.c` | 62 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/mxs-dma.c` | 754 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/ste_dma40.c` | 3451 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/apple-admac.c` | 811 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/sh/usb-dmac.c` | 748 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/fsl-edma-main.c` | 717 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/xilinx/xilinx_dma.c` | 3101 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/qcom/hidma_mgmt.c` | 196 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/img-mdc-dma.c` | 938 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/sh/rz-dmac.c` | 894 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/owl-dma.c` | 1105 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/sh/rcar-dmac.c` | 1812 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/dw-axi-dmac/dw-axi-dmac-platform.c` | 1382 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/sun6i-dma.c` | 1391 |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/sf-pdma/sf-pdma.c` | 507-511 |

Several of these (pxa_dma, sprd_dma, mmp_pdma, ti/cppi41) also support the **deprecated `#dma-channels`** property name.

### Driver with the *exact* desired fallback pattern (DT first, register fallback):
- **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/stm32/stm32-dma3.c`** -- This is the model:

```c
// Line 1806-1808
/* if dma_channels is not modified, get it from hwcfgr1 */
if (of_property_read_u32(np, "dma-channels", &ddata->dma_channels)) {
    hwcfgr = readl_relaxed(ddata->base + STM32_DMA3_HWCFGR1);
    ddata->dma_channels = FIELD_GET(G_NUM_CHANNELS, hwcfgr);
}
```

---

## 3. The specific driver most likely to be the target for this change

### **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/arm-dma350.c`** (Arm DMA-350 driver)

**Why it is the most likely target:**

1. **It reads channel count purely from a hardware register** -- There is NO `dma-channels` property lookup at all. See line 546-547:
   ```c
   reg = readl_relaxed(base + DMAINFO + DMA_BUILDCFG0);
   nchan = FIELD_GET(DMA_CFG_NUM_CHANNELS, reg) + 1;
   ```

2. **It already has full device tree binding support** -- The driver has an `of_match_table` (line 642-644) with compatible `"arm,dma-350"`, and there is a dedicated binding:
   - `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/Documentation/devicetree/bindings/dma/arm,dma-350.yaml`

3. **The binding inherits from `dma-controller.yaml`** (line 13), which inherits from `dma-common.yaml` where `dma-channels` is already defined. So the property is already valid in the binding -- the driver just does not use it.

4. **It is a relatively new driver** (2024-2025, see copyright header) -- likely still in active development and improvements.

5. **Its binding currently lacks many optional features** -- The binding at `arm,dma-350.yaml` does not mention `dma-channels` in its properties section, but it uses `unevaluatedProperties: false` (line 44) and inherits from `dma-controller.yaml` which has `additionalProperties: true`, so the property would pass validation.

### Secondary candidate: **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/pl330.c`**

Also reads channel count from hardware register `CR0` (line 1835-1837) with no `dma-channels` fallback. Has DT binding at `arm,pl330.yaml`. However, PL330 is an older, more mature driver and the change would be less likely.

---

## 4. Relevant device tree binding documentation for DMA controllers

| Binding File | For |
|-------------|-----|
| **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/Documentation/devicetree/bindings/dma/dma-common.yaml`** | **Common definitions for all DMA controllers** -- defines `dma-channels`, `dma-requests`, `dma-channel-mask` |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/Documentation/devicetree/bindings/dma/dma-controller.yaml` | Generic DMA controller node (references dma-common.yaml) |
| **`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/Documentation/devicetree/bindings/dma/arm,dma-350.yaml`** | **Arm DMA-350** -- the most likely target driver's binding |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/Documentation/devicetree/bindings/dma/arm,pl330.yaml` | ARM PL330 binding |
| `/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/Documentation/devicetree/bindings/dma/stm32/st,stm32-dma3.yaml` | STM32 DMA3 binding (the driver that already has the fallback pattern) |

---

## 5. Relevant code snippets with function names and line numbers

### A. `arm-dma350.c` -- **MOST LIKELY TARGET** (register-only, needs DT fallback)

**Function: `d350_probe()`** -- lines 526-654
```c
// Line 546-548: Channel count read from hardware register ONLY
reg = readl_relaxed(base + DMAINFO + DMA_BUILDCFG0);
nchan = FIELD_GET(DMA_CFG_NUM_CHANNELS, reg) + 1;
// ...
// Line 558: nchan stored in driver data
dmac->nchan = nchan;
```

The entire probe function (line 526-639) has **zero references** to `dma-channels`, `of_property_read_u32`, or any DT property for channel count.

### B. `stm32-dma3.c` -- **THE MODEL TO FOLLOW** (DT with register fallback)

**Function: `stm32_dma3_probe()`** -- lines ~1806-1809
```c
// Line 1806-1808: DT first, register fallback
/* if dma_channels is not modified, get it from hwcfgr1 */
if (of_property_read_u32(np, "dma-channels", &ddata->dma_channels)) {
    hwcfgr = readl_relaxed(ddata->base + STM32_DMA3_HWCFGR1);
    ddata->dma_channels = FIELD_GET(G_NUM_CHANNELS, hwcfgr);
}
```

### C. `pl330.c` -- **SECONDARY CANDIDATE** (register-only, could use DT fallback)

**Function: `read_dmac_config()`** -- lines 1822-1862
```c
// Line 1835-1838: Channel count from CR0 register
val = readl(regs + CR0) >> CR0_NUM_CHANS_SHIFT;
val &= CR0_NUM_CHANS_MASK;
val += 1;
pl330->pcfg.num_chan = val;
```

Called from `pl330_add()` (line 1959), which is called from `pl330_probe()` (line 3077). The probe function (line 3000) accesses `np = adev->dev.of_node` (line 3010) and reads other DT properties (quirks at line 3027-3029), and registers with `of_dma_controller_register` (line 3149), but never reads `dma-channels`.

---

## Summary

The **Arm DMA-350 driver** (`/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/arm-dma350.c`) is the most likely target for adding a `dma-channels` property fallback. It reads the channel count exclusively from a hardware register (`DMA_BUILDCFG0`), has full device tree binding support, and the `dma-channels` property is already defined in the common binding it inherits from. The **STM32 DMA3 driver** (`stm32-dma3.c`) provides the exact reference pattern to follow: try `of_property_read_u32(np, "dma-channels", ...)` first, and fall back to reading the hardware configuration register if the DT property is absent.
</task_result>
</task>
state.title: Find DMA driver context

state.output:
<path>/tmp/tmp.ZDBZUbpGWz/drivers/dma/arm-dma350.c</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
526: static int d350_probe(struct platform_device *pdev)
527: {
528: 	struct device *dev = &pdev->dev;
529: 	struct d350 *dmac;
530: 	void __iomem *base;
531: 	u32 reg;
532: 	int ret, nchan, dw, aw, r, p;
533: 	bool coherent, memset;
534: 
535: 	base = devm_platform_ioremap_resource(pdev, 0);
536: 	if (IS_ERR(base))
537: 		return PTR_ERR(base);
538: 
539: 	reg = readl_relaxed(base + DMAINFO + IIDR);
540: 	r = FIELD_GET(IIDR_VARIANT, reg);
541: 	p = FIELD_GET(IIDR_REVISION, reg);
542: 	if (FIELD_GET(IIDR_IMPLEMENTER, reg) != IMPLEMENTER_ARM ||
543: 	    FIELD_GET(IIDR_PRODUCTID, reg) != PRODUCTID_DMA350)
544: 		return dev_err_probe(dev, -ENODEV, "Not a DMA-350!");
545: 
546: 	reg = readl_relaxed(base + DMAINFO + DMA_BUILDCFG0);
547: 	nchan = FIELD_GET(DMA_CFG_NUM_CHANNELS, reg) + 1;
548: 	dw = 1 << FIELD_GET(DMA_CFG_DATA_WIDTH, reg);
549: 	aw = FIELD_GET(DMA_CFG_ADDR_WIDTH, reg) + 1;
550: 
551: 	dma_set_mask_and_coherent(dev, DMA_BIT_MASK(aw));
552: 	coherent = device_get_dma_attr(dev) == DEV_DMA_COHERENT;
553: 
554: 	dmac = devm_kzalloc(dev, struct_size(dmac, channels, nchan), GFP_KERNEL);
555: 	if (!dmac)
556: 		return -ENOMEM;
557: 
558: 	dmac->nchan = nchan;
559: 
560: 	reg = readl_relaxed(base + DMAINFO + DMA_BUILDCFG1);
561: 	dmac->nreq = FIELD_GET(DMA_CFG_NUM_TRIGGER_IN, reg);
562: 
563: 	dev_dbg(dev, "DMA-350 r%dp%d with %d channels, %d requests\n", r, p, dmac->nchan, dmac->nreq);
564: 
565: 	dmac->dma.dev = dev;
566: 	for (int i = min(dw, 16); i > 0; i /= 2) {
567: 		dmac->dma.src_addr_widths |= BIT(i);
568: 		dmac->dma.dst_addr_widths |= BIT(i);
569: 	}
570: 	dmac->dma.directions = BIT(DMA_MEM_TO_MEM);
571: 	dmac->dma.descriptor_reuse = true;
572: 	dmac->dma.residue_granularity = DMA_RESIDUE_GRANULARITY_BURST;
573: 	dmac->dma.device_alloc_chan_resources = d350_alloc_chan_resources;
574: 	dmac->dma.device_free_chan_resources = d350_free_chan_resources;
575: 	dma_cap_set(DMA_MEMCPY, dmac->dma.cap_mask);
576: 	dmac->dma.device_prep_dma_memcpy = d350_prep_memcpy;
577: 	dmac->dma.device_pause = d350_pause;
578: 	dmac->dma.device_resume = d350_resume;
579: 	dmac->dma.device_terminate_all = d350_terminate_all;
580: 	dmac->dma.device_synchronize = d350_synchronize;
581: 	dmac->dma.device_tx_status = d350_tx_status;
582: 	dmac->dma.device_issue_pending = d350_issue_pending;
583: 	INIT_LIST_HEAD(&dmac->dma.channels);
584: 
585: 	/* Would be nice to have per-channel caps for this... */
586: 	memset = true;
587: 	for (int i = 0; i < nchan; i++) {
588: 		struct d350_chan *dch = &dmac->channels[i];
589: 
590: 		dch->base = base + DMACH(i);
591: 		writel_relaxed(CH_CMD_CLEAR, dch->base + CH_CMD);
592: 
593: 		reg = readl_relaxed(dch->base + CH_BUILDCFG1);
594: 		if (!(FIELD_GET(CH_CFG_HAS_CMDLINK, reg))) {
595: 			dev_warn(dev, "No command link support on channel %d\n", i);
596: 			continue;
597: 		}
598: 		dch->irq = platform_get_irq(pdev, i);
599: 		if (dch->irq < 0)
600: 			return dev_err_probe(dev, dch->irq,
601: 					     "Failed to get IRQ for channel %d\n", i);
602: 
603: 		dch->has_wrap = FIELD_GET(CH_CFG_HAS_WRAP, reg);
604: 		dch->has_trig = FIELD_GET(CH_CFG_HAS_TRIGIN, reg) &
605: 				FIELD_GET(CH_CFG_HAS_TRIGSEL, reg);
606: 
607: 		/* Fill is a special case of Wrap */
608: 		memset &= dch->has_wrap;
609: 
610: 		reg = readl_relaxed(dch->base + CH_BUILDCFG0);
611: 		dch->tsz = FIELD_GET(CH_CFG_DATA_WIDTH, reg);
612: 
613: 		reg = FIELD_PREP(CH_LINK_SHAREATTR, coherent ? SHAREATTR_ISH : SHAREATTR_OSH);
614: 		reg |= FIELD_PREP(CH_LINK_MEMATTR, coherent ? MEMATTR_WB : MEMATTR_NC);
615: 		writel_relaxed(reg, dch->base + CH_LINKATTR);
616: 
617: 		dch->vc.desc_free = d350_desc_free;
618: 		vchan_init(&dch->vc, &dmac->dma);
619: 	}
620: 
621: 	if (memset) {
622: 		dma_cap_set(DMA_MEMSET, dmac->dma.cap_mask);
623: 		dmac->dma.device_prep_dma_memset = d350_prep_memset;
624: 	}
625: 
626: 	platform_set_drvdata(pdev, dmac);
627: 
628: 	ret = dma_async_device_register(&dmac->dma);
629: 	if (ret)
630: 		return dev_err_probe(dev, ret, "Failed to register DMA device\n");
631: 
632: 	return 0;
633: }
634: 
635: static void d350_remove(struct platform_device *pdev)
636: {
637: 	struct d350 *dmac = platform_get_drvdata(pdev);
638: 
639: 	dma_async_device_unregister(&dmac->dma);
640: }
641: 
642: static const struct of_device_id d350_of_match[] __maybe_unused = {
643: 	{ .compatible = "arm,dma-350" },
644: 	{}
645: };
646: MODULE_DEVICE_TABLE(of, d350_of_match);
647: 
648: static struct platform_driver d350_driver = {
649: 	.driver = {
650: 		.name = "arm-dma350",
651: 		.of_match_table = of_match_ptr(d350_of_match),
652: 	},
653: 	.probe = d350_probe,
654: 	.remove = d350_remove,
655: };

(Showing lines 526-655 of 660. Use offset=656 to continue.)
</content>

display.text:
# SPDX-License-Identifier: (GPL-2.0-only OR BSD-2-Clause)
%YAML 1.2
---
$id: http://devicetree.org/schemas/dma/arm,dma-350.yaml#
$schema: http://devicetree.org/meta-schemas/core.yaml#

title: Arm CoreLink DMA-350 Controller

maintainers:
  - Robin Murphy <robin.murphy@arm.com>

allOf:
  - $ref: dma-controller.yaml#

properties:
  compatible:
    const: arm,dma-350

  reg:
    items:
      - description: Base and size of the full register map

  interrupts:
    minItems: 1
    items:
      - description: Channel 0 interrupt

part.text:
The change is applied. Now the driver at `drivers/dma/arm-dma350.c:547` first checks the `dma-channels` device tree property, falling back to reading the `DMA_BUILDCFG0` hardware register when the property is absent. The register is still read unconditionally since it also provides `dw` and `aw` fields.

The `dma-channels` property is already defined in `Documentation/devicetree/bindings/dma/dma-common.yaml` (inherited by the driver's binding via `dma-controller.yaml`), so no binding change is needed.
